fault_hypothesis: On line 59, the mean is computed by dividing total by `window` instead of `len(recent)`. When fewer values exist than the window size, this inflates the denominator and produces a value smaller than the true average of the available samples.

constraints.intent: mean must equal total divided by the number of available samples, not by window
after computing mean, mean times the actual sample count must equal total

spec.expr: len(recent__1) >= window or abs(mean__1 - total__1 / len(recent__1)) < 1e-9

constraints.intent: before using mean in the return, it should equal the true average of available samples when fewer samples than window exist

spec.expr: __cbfl_result is None or abs(__cbfl_result - round(sum(values[-window:]) / len(values[-window:]), 2)) < 1e-9

constraints.intent: the returned value must match the mean of only the available recent samples
